pr.title: Rename gm_17C0 to gmregclear based on assert
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Renamed the game-mode source/header pair from address-based gm_17C0 to the assert-derived name gmregclear, and propagated the new filename through build configuration, split metadata, includes, aggregate game-mode header exports, and a comment reference. No code behavior appears to have changed; this was a naming/organization cleanup preserving the existing nonmatching object status.
what_changed: Renamed split entry `melee/gm/gm_17C0.c` to `melee/gm/gmregclear.c` while keeping the same section ranges, including `.text` start `0x8017C0C8` end `0x8018325C`.
Renamed `src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.c` to `src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c` and changed its self-include from `gm_17C0.h` to `gmregclear.h`.
Renamed `src/melee/gm/gm_17C0.h` to `src/melee/gm/gmregclear.h` and updated the include guard from `MELEE_GM_17C0_H` to `MELEE_gmregclear_H`.
Replaced includes of `gm_17C0.h` with `gmregclear.h` in `gm_1601.h`, `grcorneria.c`, and `vi0401.c`.
Updated `gm_unsplit.h` IWYU export includes to export `gmregclear.h` instead of `gm_17C0.h`; the hunk also aligned spacing around the IWYU comments.
Updated an `lbbgflash.c` file comment so the list of game-mode callers names `gmregclear.c` instead of `gm_17C0.c`.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated across both build inputs (`configure.py`) and split metadata (`config/GALE01/splits.txt`), avoiding a common partial-rename failure where the source compiles locally but object/split bookkeeping still uses the old address-based name.
The PR preserved the object's `NonMatching` status in `configure.py`, correctly treating this as a naming improvement rather than implying any matching progress.
Updating direct includes, the aggregate `gm_unsplit.h` export header, and documentation comments made the new name searchable and consistent across call sites.

The title records the evidence source for the name: the rename was based on an assert. The diff excerpt does not show the assert itself, so the exact string is not independently verifiable from this slice.
decomp_lessons: When an assert or embedded string reveals a better module name, prefer replacing address-placeholder filenames like `gm_17C0` with the recovered semantic name, but update all build/split/include references in the same PR.
A file-level rename in this repo needs coordination between `src/...`, `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, and `configure.py`; changing only the C/H files is not enough.
Keep matching classification separate from naming cleanup: a renamed file can remain `NonMatching` until its functions actually match.
Aggregate headers such as `gm_unsplit.h` must be kept in sync with renamed module headers, especially when they use `IWYU pragma: export`.
Comments that mention module names are useful search surfaces and should be updated during renames, even when they do not affect compilation.
naming_conventions: `gm_17C0` was an address-derived placeholder name for a game-mode module; `gmregclear` is the recovered semantic module name used for both `.c` and `.h`.
The include path convention after the rename is `#include "gm/gmregclear.h"` for local project includes and `<melee/gm/gmregclear.h>` in exported game-mode headers.
The split/config object path follows the source path without the `src/` prefix: `melee/gm/gmregclear.c`.
The new header guard in the diff is `MELEE_gmregclear_H`; this is evidence from the PR, though it is less stylistically consistent than the old all-caps `MELEE_GM_17C0_H` guard.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function-level matching tactics are shown in the slice.
The PR is explicitly a rename of a still-`NonMatching` object in `configure.py`, indicating no matching-status change.
The split section addresses were preserved, which is the right tactic for a pure file rename: keep object layout metadata identical while changing only the module path.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining references to `gm_17C0` after the rename to catch stale comments, includes, or scripts.
Look for the assert string that motivated `gmregclear` to document the source of the recovered module name more explicitly.
Consider normalizing the new header guard style if the project expects all-caps guards such as `MELEE_GMREGCLEAR_H`.
Check whether additional symbols inside `gmregclear.c` can now be renamed using the same assert-derived context.
